note: Cross-cutting biblical-imagery/typology overlay. Keys are existing article ids; this registry never recategorizes an article. See working/MOTIFS_DESIGN.md.

elemental.name: Elemental & Substance
elemental.blurb: Images drawn from the raw stuff of creation — water, fire, light, blood, oil.

cosmic.name: Cosmic & Geographic
cosmic.blurb: Places and spaces that carry theological weight — mountain, wilderness, garden, sea, city.

flora.name: Flora
flora.blurb: Plant imagery — vine, tree, seed, harvest, branch.

fauna.name: Fauna
fauna.blurb: Creatures used as symbols — lamb, lion, serpent, dove, dragon.

human.name: Human Roles & Relationships
human.blurb: Relational images — shepherd, king, father, bridegroom, redeemer, servant.

body.name: Body & Garment
body.blurb: The body and what clothes it — heart, hand, name, garment, crown.

cultic.name: Cultic Objects & Architecture
cultic.blurb: Worship furniture and sacred space — bread, cup, temple, altar, veil, lampstand.

pattern.name: Pattern (Event-as-Type)
pattern.blurb: Redemptive events that recur as a shape — exodus, exile, flood, sabbath rest.

number.name: Number
number.blurb: Symbolic quantities — seven, twelve, forty, three.

color.name: Color
color.blurb: Symbolic hues — white, scarlet, blue, purple.

purple.gloss: Royalty and costly splendor — the hidden kingship of the crucified Lord.

nakedness.gloss: Exposure and shame from sin — covered by God's gracious clothing.

remnant.gloss: The faithful few God preserves through judgment to carry his purpose.

typology.shadow: God preserves a faithful remnant of Israel through flood, apostasy, and exile to keep his promise alive.
typology.fulfillment: The believing remnant of Israel, into which Gentiles are grafted, becomes the church — God's people chosen by grace.

scroll.gloss: God's enduring written word and decree — unsealed at last by the Lamb.

door.gloss: The one point of access to God's presence — Christ the door to life.

mustard-seed.gloss: Tiny beginnings yielding the kingdom's vast, unstoppable growth.

fig-tree.gloss: Fruitfulness and covenant peace — or barrenness ripe for judgment.

garden.gloss: The watered place of God's presence — Eden lost, paradise restored.

typology.shadow: Eden, the garden-sanctuary of God's presence, is lost when sin drives humanity out and bars the tree of life.
typology.fulfillment: Through the risen Christ, communion is restored in the new creation — a garden-city with the river and tree of life.

name.gloss: God's revealed character, presence, and renown — now borne by Jesus.

bridegroom.gloss: God as the husband who weds himself to his people in covenant love.

typology.shadow: The LORD betroths himself to Israel as a husband, grieved by her unfaithfulness yet pledging to wed her forever.
typology.fulfillment: Christ the bridegroom gives himself for his bride the church, to present her holy and to wed her at the marriage of the Lamb.

passover.gloss: The lamb's blood that turns away judgment and works redemption.

typology.shadow: The blood of the Passover lamb on the doorposts shields Israel from the destroyer and frees them from Egypt.
typology.fulfillment: Christ, our Passover lamb, is sacrificed so his blood shields his people from judgment and accomplishes the true exodus from sin and death.

fire.gloss: God's holy, refining, and judging presence.

light.gloss: God's self-revelation, holiness, and life over against darkness.

darkness.gloss: Ignorance, evil, judgment, and the absence of God.

blood.gloss: Life poured out; the price and means of atonement.

typology.shadow: Sacrificial blood applied to make atonement and ratify covenant.
typology.fulfillment: The blood of Christ, shed once for all, cleanses and ratifies the new covenant.

oil.gloss: Anointing, consecration, and the Holy Spirit.

leaven.gloss: A small influence that permeates the whole — of sin or of the kingdom.

stone.gloss: Permanence, witness, and the building of God's house.

rock.gloss: God as refuge and foundation; the Rock that gave water.

typology.shadow: The rock at Horeb struck to give Israel water in the wilderness.
typology.fulfillment: Christ, the spiritual Rock who followed them and gives living water.

zion.gloss: The city of God; the dwelling of his people, earthly and heavenly.

wilderness.gloss: Place of testing, dependence, and encounter with God.

desert.gloss: Desolation and the proving ground of faith.

city.gloss: Human community — Babylon in rebellion, Jerusalem in covenant.

babylon.gloss: Proud, idolatrous world-power set against God.

well.gloss: Source of life and place of providential meeting.

spring.gloss: Fresh, living source of life from God.

fountain.gloss: God himself as the inexhaustible source of life.

east.gloss: Direction of exile and of return; the rising of light.

vine.gloss: Israel as God's planting; Christ as the true vine.

typology.shadow: Israel planted as the LORD's vineyard, called to bear fruit.
typology.fulfillment: Christ the true vine, in whom his people abide and bear fruit.

olive-tree.gloss: Israel, peace, the Spirit's anointing, and the grafted-in nations.

olive.gloss: Fruit of peace, light, and anointing.

tree-of-life.gloss: Access to unending life in God's presence.

typology.shadow: The tree of life in Eden, barred after the fall.
typology.fulfillment: Restored access to the tree of life in the new creation through Christ.

branch.gloss: The messianic shoot from David's stump.

typology.shadow: A Branch promised from the line of Jesse and David.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus the righteous Branch, the King who reigns in righteousness.

harvest.gloss: Ingathering of God's people and the judgment at the end.

typology.shadow: The agricultural harvest and its firstfruits offered to God.
typology.fulfillment: The final ingathering of the redeemed and the separating judgment.

wheat.gloss: The righteous gathered into God's barn.

tares.gloss: The wicked growing among the righteous until judgment.

lily.gloss: God's care, beauty, and provision for his people.

lamb.gloss: Innocent victim; the Lamb who takes away sin.

typology.shadow: The Passover and sacrificial lambs whose blood spared and atoned.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus, the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world.

lion.gloss: Royal strength and judgment; the Lion of Judah; also the adversary.

sheep.gloss: God's people — dependent, prone to stray, in need of a shepherd.

shepherd.gloss: God and his appointed king as shepherd of the flock.

typology.shadow: The LORD as Israel's shepherd and the promise of one shepherd from David.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus the good shepherd who lays down his life for the sheep.

serpent.gloss: Deception, the curse, and the ancient enemy.

dragon.gloss: Chaos and Satan, the great enemy of God's people.

dove.gloss: Peace, innocence, and the descending Spirit.

eagle.gloss: Swiftness, renewal, and God's protective bearing of his people.

goat.gloss: Sin offering and the separating of the unrighteous in judgment.

fish.gloss: Abundance and the gathering of people in mission.

locust.gloss: Devouring judgment and the day of the LORD.

leviathan.gloss: Untamable chaos-monster subdued by God alone.

behemoth.gloss: Primeval creature displaying the Creator's power.

wolf.gloss: Predatory danger — false teachers and enemies of the flock.

scapegoat.gloss: The goat bearing sin away into the wilderness.

typology.shadow: On the Day of Atonement, sins confessed over the goat sent away.
typology.fulfillment: Christ bearing his people's sin away, removing it as far as east from west.

king.gloss: God's reign and the Davidic king who represents it.

father.gloss: God's fatherly authority, care, and adopting love.

bride.gloss: God's covenant people, betrothed and made faithful.

husband.gloss: God as the faithful husband of his covenant people.

redeemer.gloss: The kinsman who buys back the enslaved and avenges the wronged.

typology.shadow: The kinsman-redeemer (goel) who redeems family, land, and life.
typology.fulfillment: Christ our Redeemer, who buys his people back with his own blood.

judge.gloss: God who rules, vindicates the righteous, and condemns evil.

priest.gloss: The mediator who offers sacrifice and intercedes.

typology.shadow: The Aaronic and Melchizedek priesthoods mediating between God and people.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus the great high priest, who ever lives to intercede.

prophet.gloss: The mouthpiece of God who speaks his word.

typology.shadow: Moses and the prophets, and the promise of a prophet like Moses.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus the prophet to whom all must listen.

adoption.gloss: Being made sons and heirs of God by grace.

heart.gloss: The inner person — the seat of will, thought, and worship.

hand.gloss: Power, agency, and the active work of God.

circumcision.gloss: The covenant sign cut in flesh; the call for a cut heart.

typology.shadow: Circumcision of the flesh as the sign of the Abrahamic covenant.
typology.fulfillment: Circumcision of the heart by the Spirit, made without hands in Christ.

crown.gloss: Honor, sovereignty, and the reward of the faithful.

bread.gloss: God's provision and sustenance; the bread of life.

typology.shadow: Manna in the wilderness and the showbread in the holy place.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus the bread of life, given for the world.

wine.gloss: Joy and gladness; the blood of the covenant; also the cup of wrath.

cup.gloss: One's appointed portion — of blessing or of wrath.

feast.gloss: Covenant fellowship and the joy of God's presence.

typology.shadow: Israel's appointed feasts foreshadowing redemption's stages.
typology.fulfillment: Fulfilled in Christ and consummated at the marriage supper of the Lamb.

banquet.gloss: The messianic feast of the kingdom of God.

typology.shadow: The Jerusalem temple as the place of God's presence.
typology.fulfillment: Christ's body, then his people and the new Jerusalem, as God's temple.

tabernacle.gloss: God tabernacling in the midst of his people.

typology.shadow: The wilderness tent where God's glory dwelt among Israel.
typology.fulfillment: The Word made flesh who tabernacled among us; God dwelling with man.

tent.gloss: Pilgrim dwelling; the frailty and transience of earthly life.

altar.gloss: The place of sacrifice and of access to God.

lamp.gloss: God's word and the witness of his people as light.

typology.shadow: The veil shutting off the most holy place from the people.
typology.fulfillment: The veil torn at Christ's death, opening a new and living way to God.

ark.gloss: The ark of the covenant — God's throne and presence.

typology.shadow: The mercy seat over the ark where atonement blood was sprinkled.
typology.fulfillment: Christ the mercy seat (propitiation) where God and sinners meet.

yoke.gloss: Servitude and burden, or willing discipleship under Christ.

book.gloss: God's record and decree; the book of life.

seal.gloss: Ownership, authentication, and the security of the Spirit.

sword.gloss: Judgment, war, and the piercing word of God.

manna.gloss: Bread from heaven sustaining the pilgrim people.

typology.shadow: Daily manna feeding Israel in the wilderness.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus the true bread from heaven who gives eternal life.

cornerstone.gloss: The tested foundation stone of God's building.

typology.shadow: The stone the builders rejected, made the chief cornerstone.
typology.fulfillment: Christ the cornerstone on whom the whole structure is joined.

exodus.gloss: Deliverance from bondage into covenant freedom.

typology.shadow: The LORD redeeming Israel out of Egypt through the sea.
typology.fulfillment: Christ accomplishing the greater exodus, redeeming his people from sin.

typology.shadow: Israel driven from the land into Babylon for covenant breaking.
typology.fulfillment: Humanity's exile from Eden ended by Christ, who brings the people home.

flood.gloss: Judgment on a corrupt world and salvation through it.

typology.shadow: The flood destroying the world while Noah's family was carried safely through.
typology.fulfillment: Baptism into Christ, who carries his people through judgment to new life.

sabbath.gloss: God's rest, hallowed for his people.

typology.shadow: The weekly sabbath rest commanded for Israel.
typology.fulfillment: The sabbath rest that remains for God's people, entered through Christ.

rest.gloss: The promised rest of God — land, peace, and his presence.

typology.shadow: The rest of Canaan that Joshua gave Israel, yet incomplete.
typology.fulfillment: The eternal rest into which Christ leads his people.

brazen-serpent.gloss: The lifted-up sign that brings life to the dying.

typology.shadow: The bronze serpent lifted on a pole; all who looked lived.
typology.fulfillment: The Son of Man lifted up, that whoever believes has eternal life.

ladder.gloss: The connection opened between heaven and earth.

typology.shadow: Jacob's ladder with angels ascending and descending.
typology.fulfillment: Christ the way by whom heaven is opened and access to God is given.

rainbow.gloss: Sign of God's covenant mercy after judgment.

adam.gloss: The first man and head of the human race.

typology.shadow: Adam, in whom all die, a type of the one to come.
typology.fulfillment: Christ the last Adam, the life-giving Spirit and head of a new humanity.

melchizedek.gloss: Priest-king of Salem who blessed Abraham.

typology.shadow: Melchizedek, priest forever without recorded genealogy.
typology.fulfillment: Christ, a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

noah.gloss: The righteous man saved through the flood.

typology.shadow: Noah and his household carried through judgment in the ark.
typology.fulfillment: Christ, in whom the church is carried safely through judgment.

scarlet.gloss: Sin and the blood of sacrifice.

crimson.gloss: Deep-dyed sin, cleansed white as snow.

blue.gloss: Heaven, and the heavenly origin of the law.

water.gloss: Life and chaos; God brings salvation, cleansing, and the Spirit through water.

mountain.gloss: The meeting-place of heaven and earth; where God descends and his kingdom is unveiled.

sea.gloss: The chaotic deep that only the Creator can bound; abolished in the new creation.

servant.gloss: The LORD's chosen instrument; fulfilled in Christ the suffering Servant.

typology.shadow: Moses, David, and faithful Israel bore the title "servant of the LORD," and Isaiah foretold a Servant who would bear the people's sins.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus is the true Servant who, taking the form of a servant, fulfills Isaiah 53 by bearing sin and justifying many through obedient suffering.

firstborn.gloss: The preeminent heir consecrated to God; fulfilled in Christ, firstborn over all.

typology.shadow: Israel and the Davidic king are named God's firstborn — heir and ruler — yet the privilege is repeatedly forfeited or reassigned.
typology.fulfillment: Christ is the true firstborn: heir of all creation, firstborn from the dead, and elder brother of the redeemed who never forfeits his birthright.

twelve.gloss: The number of God's complete covenant people: twelve tribes and twelve apostles.

living-water.gloss: The fresh, flowing water of life; God himself, given by Christ and the Spirit.

wind.gloss: One word for wind, breath, and Spirit: God's unseen, sovereign, life-giving power.

the-way.gloss: The path of life before God; Christ is himself the way to the Father.

vineyard.gloss: God's cultivated people, planted and tended to bear the fruit of righteousness.

seed.gloss: The buried grain that dies to bear fruit, and the promised offspring fulfilled in Christ.

typology.shadow: God promised the seed of the woman to crush the serpent and Abraham's seed to bless the nations, and the buried grain pictured life from death.
typology.fulfillment: Christ is the singular promised Seed who, like a grain of wheat, dies and rises to bear a great harvest and make all who are his Abraham's offspring.

firstfruits.gloss: The first of the harvest offered to God; a pledge that the full harvest will follow.

typology.shadow: Israel waved the first sheaf of the barley harvest before the LORD, offered before and guaranteeing the full harvest.
typology.fulfillment: The risen Christ is the firstfruits of those who sleep, his resurrection securing the full harvest of the resurrection of his people.

thorns.gloss: The emblem of the curse and futility of sin; worn as a crown by the suffering King.

garment.gloss: Clothing as righteousness and standing before God; believers are clothed in Christ.

typology.shadow: God clothed Adam and Eve with garments of skin at the cost of life, and Israel longed for clean priestly vestments and the robe of righteousness to cover sin.
typology.fulfillment: In Christ believers "put on Christ," exchanging filthy rags for the robe of salvation, made white in the blood of the Lamb.

lampstand.gloss: The golden lamp of continual light in the sanctuary; fulfilled in the Church bearing Christ's light.

typology.shadow: The seven-branched golden menorah gave continual light in the Holy Place, sustained by oil before the LORD.
typology.fulfillment: The Church is the golden lampstand among which the risen Christ walks, bearing his light to the world by the oil of the Spirit.

table.gloss: God hosting his people; communion and covenant fellowship at the LORD's table.

day-of-the-lord.gloss: God's decisive day of judgment and salvation, fulfilled in the return of Christ.

jubilee.gloss: The fiftieth-year release of debts and slaves, fulfilled in Christ's great liberation.

typology.shadow: Every fiftieth year Israel was to sound the trumpet, release debts, free slaves, and restore each family to its inheritance, all flowing from the Day of Atonement.
typology.fulfillment: Jesus proclaims the true and final Jubilee, releasing captives from sin, forgiving the debt we owe, and restoring our lost inheritance through his atonement.

warrior.gloss: God as the mighty champion who fights to save his people; victorious in Christ.

arm-of-the-lord.gloss: God's outstretched saving power; revealed and embodied in the crucified Christ.

typology.shadow: God redeemed Israel from Egypt with an outstretched arm and bared his holy arm to work salvation.
typology.fulfillment: The arm of the LORD is revealed in the Suffering Servant, Jesus Christ, God's saving power displayed through the cross.

three.gloss: Divine completeness and decisive action, marked supremely by the third-day resurrection.

forty.gloss: A complete season of testing and discipline leading to a new beginning.
